verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 subunit beta) is a core catalytic subunit of the mitochondrial ATP synthase complex (Complex V). It localizes exclusively to the inner mitochondrial membrane as part of the F1 head domain and does not reach the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. While ATP synthase is a membrane protein, it is a resident of the inner mitochondrial membrane—an endomembrane compartment internal to the cell. There is no documented mechanism for ATP5F1B to transit to or assemble at the plasma membrane under any physiological condition. Surface accessibility would require plasma membrane targeting and either a transmembrane domain or stable partnership with a surface-anchored protein, neither of which applies to this mitochondrial matrix-facing catalytic subunit.
